name: lcx-report-bug
tagline: Routes LazyCodex/Codex CLI bugs to the owning GitHub repo, gathers source+runtime evidence, then files a labeled issue or PR via gh.
verdict: No malicious behavior found: no credential reads, no obfuscation, no manipulation of the reviewing agent, no destructive git ops. Two real risks: it files public GitHub issues/PRs autonomously with no explicit user-confirmation gate on the gh path (only the browser/computer-use fallbacks require visual confirmation before submit), and its templates push 'Rel

highlights.summary: Egress to GitHub is the stated purpose, but the Issue/PR templates (lines 134-139, 158-159) ask for 'Relevant config' and raw evidence/logs with no redaction or secret-scrubbing instruction before pos

The primary `gh issue create`/`gh pr create` path (steps 9-11, lines 101-114) has no explicit 'confirm with user before submitting' gate; only the browser-use and computer-use fallbacks (lines 244, 25
No negative-trigger guidance is given (e.g., not for feature requests, not for unrelated-repo bugs); description only lists positive triggers.
